payload.text: 完善建筑物建造功能，点击建造按钮后，弹出确认弹窗，确认是否消耗灵石建造，点击确认开始建造；开始建造后，增加一个取消按钮点击取消，弹出取消弹窗，确认是否取消，点击确认，返还灵石，取消建造

**Adding Confirmation Dialogues**

I'm currently focused on the building construction feature. I've just implemented a confirmation dialog that appears when the "Start Building" button is clicked. This dialog now displays the exact cost in spirit stones, as we discussed. Next, I'll need to work on the "Start Building" functionality after confirmation.


**Implementing Construction Lifecycle**

I've got the construction process moving forward now. After the initial confirmation, buildings properly decrement turns each cycle. I've also added a "Cancel" button, which, as expected, prompts a second confirmation dialog. Finally, confirmed cancellations refund spirit stones and reset the building state, all handled within the relevant components, using the `GameState` and building interfaces defined in `types.ts`.


**Structuring Building Management Logic**

I'm structuring the code for building management. I'm focusing on the `App.tsx`, `ClanManagement.tsx`, and `BuildingsTab.tsx` components. Specifically, I'm working to implement functions that handle the addition and cancellation of buildings, including spirit stone deductions and refunds. I will be updating state to include confirmation modals, and building state management.


**Enhancing Modal Consistency**

I'm focused on the UI elements. Specifically, I'm integrating the new confirmation modal, reusing the parchment/ink aesthetic from other modals. I've ensured a consistent look, drawing from `EventModal` and `WarehouseTab` for styling. I've also added subtle fade-in animations. I'm reviewing the state management logic in `App.tsx` and verifying it functions to reflect turn logic.


**Updating Turn Logic**

I'm currently updating the `handleNextTurn` function in `App.tsx` to progress building construction. I've implemented logic to decrement turns and update building states, adding logs upon completion. Now, I'm refactoring `App.tsx` and related components like `ClanManagement.tsx` and `BuildingsTab.tsx`, ensuring proper prop passing and modal rendering. I will be updating state to include confirmation modals, and building state management.


**Reviewing Data Consistency**

I'm focused on the data structure for buildings. I've re-reviewed the building data object in `types.ts`, and checked the properties `level`, `assignedMemberId`, `isFinished`, `turnsRemaining`, and `activeProduction`. Everything appears to be consistent now. I'm satisfied with the data and ready to move on.

entries.description: Implemented building construction progression logic in handleNextTurn, and added handleAddBuilding and handleCancelBuilding with refund logic.
Updated ClanManagement to include onCancelBuilding prop and pass it down to BuildingsTab.
Implemented confirmation modals for starting and canceling building construction, including a refund mechanism.

payload.text: 将SmithingTab移动到components/ClanManagement/Tabs/BuildingsTabs文件夹下；新增藏经阁、炼器坊、修炼室的Tab文件

**Restructuring Clan Tabs**

I'm currently focused on restructuring the Clan Management tabs. My goal is to move `SmithingTab.tsx` into a `components/ClanManagement/Tabs/BuildingsTabs` location. Although the user mentioned a `BuildingsTabs` folder, the folder likely follows the existing structure to be simply `Tabs`. This is the primary focus of my current thinking.


**Refining Tab Organization**

I'm now thinking about how to organize the new tabs, specifically the ones for the buildings: Library, Smithy, and Cultivation Room. It seems logical to create a `BuildingsTabs` subfolder within `components/ClanManagement/Tabs/` to house these building-specific tabs, as the prompt suggests. I'm also considering renaming the existing `SmithingTab` to align it with the building name.


**Clarifying Building Tab Roles**

I've been further refining my understanding of the tab organization. It seems the existing `BuildingsTab.tsx` handles building construction, while the new tabs will manage those buildings. My revised plan involves creating a `BuildingsTabs` folder and moving `SmithingTab.tsx` (renaming it to `SmithyTab.tsx`), and adding `LibraryTab.tsx` and `CultivationRoomTab.tsx` there. I'm also considering where `AlchemyTab.tsx` should reside.


**Organizing Building Tab Content**

I'm now clarifying the roles of the new building tabs. `LibraryTab.tsx` will manage family methods and heritage, while `CultivationRoomTab.tsx` will handle member assignments. Furthermore, I will create `AlchemyRoomTab.tsx` as a new building tab. And I have now consolidated both the `SmithyTab.tsx` and `AlchemyTab.tsx` into the `BuildingsTabs` folder, per the user's prompt, and included the new `LibraryTab.tsx` and `CultivationRoomTab.tsx` tabs.


**Implementing Tab Updates**

I'm now focused on updating `ClanManagement.tsx` to handle the new tabs. I plan to incorporate logic for displaying `Library` and `CultivationRoom` tabs, adding them alongside the existing `Alchemy` and `Smithy` tabs. I'm also reviewing `BUILDING_TYPES` to ensure accurate integration with the new building tabs.


**Extending Tab Functionality**

I'm now implementing the user's suggestions more concretely. I'm focusing on incorporating the new tabs, including `CultivationRoomTab`, `LibraryTab`, `SmithyTab`, and `AlchemyRoomTab`, into `ClanManagement.tsx`. I will update the imports and rendering logic, extending the `activeTab` type and `onAssignBuilding` function. I intend to ensure the existing functionality remains intact, while enhancing it to accommodate the new building tabs and their specific responsibilities, specifically for adding both Library and CultivationRoom.


**Coding the Building Tabs**

I'm now fully immersed in coding the new building tabs. I've updated `ClanManagement.tsx` to handle the tab updates, including imports, and the new state types. My primary focus is on implementing the `LibraryTab` and `CultivationRoomTab` logic. I'm also implementing tabs for `AlchemyRoomTab` and `SmithyTab` after incorporating user suggestions.


**Refactoring File Locations**

I'm now fully focused on creating the new building tabs and moving existing ones. Given the user's explicit request, I'm now certain of the files to update, with the addition of renaming and moving files to ensure consistency. The final list includes updated versions of `AlchemyRoomTab`, `SmithyTab`, and new files for `LibraryTab` and `CultivationRoomTab`. These updates will involve moving the `SmithingTab` into the `BuildingsTabs` folder as well.


**Analyzing File Management**

I've realized I don't need to explicitly "delete" files. If I alter imports in `ClanManagement.tsx`, orphaned files are functionally removed. I've confirmed `AlchemyTab.tsx` exists. I'm now setting up the file structure and creating the new building tabs based on the specified logic for both Library and CultivationRoom, very similar in structure to Smithy and Alchemy.

payload.text:
**Analyzing Modal Placement**

I've pinpointed the "Confirm Build" modal within `components/ClanManagement/Tabs/BuildingsTab.tsx`. My current focus is figuring out how to adjust its vertical position by 50px. I'm exploring the code to identify the relevant styling or positioning properties that control its current location.


**Implementing Vertical Shift**

Okay, I've got it. The modals are centered using a `flex` container. To move the "Confirm Build" modal upwards, I'll add `-translate-y-[50px]` to the inner `div`'s class. I've re-examined the code in `BuildingsTab.tsx` and can confirm this approach will work, affecting only the target modal. The same technique can be used for the cancel modal.


**Modifying Modal Position**

I'm now implementing the vertical shift for both the "Confirm Build" and "Confirm Cancel" modals. I'm adding `-translate-y-[50px]` to their respective inner `div` classes. I've confirmed this approach in `BuildingsTab.tsx`. I'll also ensure that no other modals in the relevant area require a similar adjustment, per the user's specific request for the "Building Decree" modal in the provided image. My next step will be to test these changes.


**Confirming Implementation Details**

I'm solidifying the implementation of the vertical shift. I've re-reviewed `BuildingsTab.tsx` and am focused on integrating the necessary CSS classes, specifically adding `-translate-y-[50px]` to the modal containers. My next step will be to test and verify these changes within the development environment.

entries.description: 将建造和取消建造的确认弹窗向上移动 50px，以优化视觉平衡。
